preamble: The shift from a high nasalized vowel [ĩ] to a low nasalized vowel [ã] represents a process of lowering, often seen in the historical development of nasal vowels where nasalization triggers a downward shift in vowel height.

examples.note: Historical development from Latin i: + nasal consonant, passing through stages like [ɛ̃] before reaching [ã] in some dialects or mergers.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
